(a route of eleven days from Chore way of Mount Seir, up to Kades Barne).

The Lord our God spoke to us at Choreb ing, “Let it be sufficient for you to live at mountain.

Turn, and depart, you, and go int mountain of the Amorrites and to all those live around Araba, to mountain and plain a the southwest and seacoast, the land of Chana and Anti-Lebanon, as far as the great Euphrates.
See, I have given the land before when you have gone in, inherit the land that I to your fathers, to Abraam and Isaak and Iakob, t to them and to their offspring after them.”
And I said to you in that time, saying: “I not be able alone to bear you.
The Lord God has multiplied you, and look, you are t like the stars of the sky in number.
May the L the God of your fathers, add to you, as you a thousand times and bless you, as he has spoke you!
How will I be able alone to bear your h ship and your substance and your disputes? 1 sign for yourselves men, wise and discerning prudent for your tribes, and I will appoint the your leaders.”

And I took from your men, wise and discer and prudent, and appointed them to be lea over you—officers of thousands and officer hundreds and officers of fifties and officers of and recorders for your judges.
And I comm ed your judges at that time, saying: “Give a hearing among your brothers, and judge rig between a man and between his brother and tween his guest.

You shall not recognize the son when judging: like the small so you shall j the great; you shall not shrink from the face person, for the judgment is God’s. And the ma if it be too hard for you, you will bring it to and I will hear it.”
And I commanded you in time all the things that you should do.

And after we departed from Choreb, went through all that great and terrible wilder that you saw, by route of the mountain of Amorrite, just as the Lord our God had comm ed us, and we came as far as Kades Barne.

And all of you came to me and said, “Let us send men ahead of us, and let them explore the land for us, and let them report to us a decision regarding the route through which we shall go up on it and the cities into which we will come into them.”
And the matter was pleasing to me, and I took from you twelve men, one man a tribe.
And after they had set out, they went up into the mountain and went as far as Cluster Ravine and surveyed it.
And they took in their hands some of the fruit of the land and brought it down to us and said, “The land is good that the Lord our God is giving us.”

And you did not want to go up. And you disobeyed the word of the Lord your God, 27and you grumbled in your tents and said, “It is because the Lord hates us that he brought us out of the land of Egypt, to give us into the hands of the Amorrites to destroy us utterly.

Where are we going up? Our brothers have disquieted our hearts, saying, ‘A great and numerous nation and more powerful than we, and cities, large and walled up to the sky! Nay, even sons of giants we have seen there!’ ”
And I said to you, “Do not be alarmed, neither be afraid of them.

The Lord your God, who goes before you, he will fight them together with you, according to all which he did for you in the land of Egypt 31and in this wilderness which you saw, how the Lord your God nursed you, as some person would nurse his son, along all the way that you went until you came to this place.

And in this matter you did not believe the Lord your God, 33who went before you on the way to choose a place for you, leading you by fire at night, showing you the route whereby you should go upon it, and in a cloud by day.”
AndtheLordheardthesoundofyourwords and, being provoked, he swore, saying:
“If one of these men shall see this good land that I swore to your fathers!
Except Chaleb son of Iephonne, he shall see it, and to him I will give the land on which he trod, and to his sons, because he followed closely the things of the Lord.”
Even with me the Lord was angry on your account, saying, “Not even you shall enter there.
Iesous son of Naue, who stands beside you, he shall enter there; strengthen him, for he will take possession of it for Israel.
And every young child, who today does not know good or evil, they shall enter there, and to them I will give it, and they shall inherit it.
And when you have turned, march into the wilderness, by the route toward the Red Sea.”

Andyouansweredandsaidtome,“Wehave sinned against the Lord our God! Let us go up and fight, in accordance with all that the Lord our God commanded us.” And, each of you, when you had strapped on your battle gear and had gathered, went up into the mountain.
And the Lord said to me, “Say to them, ‘You shall not go up, neither shall you fight, for I am not with you, and you 148 deutero shall not be wiped out before your enemies.’ ”
And I spoke to you, but you did not listen to me. And you transgressed the word of the Lord, and aby forcea you went up into the mountain.
And the Amorrite who lived in that mountain came out to meet you and chased you as bees might do. And they were wounding you from Seir as far as Herma.
And when you sat down, you were weeping before the Lord, and the Lord neither listened to your voice nor paid you any attention.
And you stayed at Kades for many days, however many days you stayed.
And after we had turned, we departed into the wilderness by way of the Red Sea, as the Lord had told me, and went around Mount Seir for many days.
And the Lord said to me:

“Let it be sufficient for you to go around this mountain. Turn therefore to the north, 4and command the people, saying: You are passing through the borders of your brothers, the sons of Esau, who live in Seir. And they will be afraid of you, and they will be very cautious.
Do not engage in battle with them, for I will not even give you a foot’s pace of their land, since I have given Mount Seir to Esau as an allotment.
Purchase food from them, and you will eat, and you shall also take water by measure from them for money, and you will drink.

For the Lord our God has blessed you in every work of your hands. Consider how you crossed this great and terrible wilderness. Look, for forty years the Lord your God has been with you; you have not been in need of a thing.”

And we passed by our brothers, the sons of Esau who live in Seir, along the route of the Araba, from Ailon and from Gasion Gaber. And after we had turned, we went along the wilderness route of Moab, 9and the Lord said to me: “Do not act with enmity against the Moabites, and do not engage in battle with them, for I will not give you any of their land as an allotment, for I have given Aroer to the sons of Lot to inherit.”
(The erstwhile Ommin—a nation large and numerous and mighty like the Enakim—had settled in it.
Like the Enakim, these also shall be reckoned as Rephain, and the Moabites name them Ommin.

And the Chorrite had formerly lain couched in Seir, and the sons of Esau destroyed them and wiped them out from before them, and they were settled in their place, as Israel did with the land of its inheritance that the Lord gave them.)
“Now then, rise up, and depart, and go alongside the ravine of Zared.” So we passed along the ravine of Zared.

And the days which we had gone alongside from Kades Barne until we passed along the ravine of Zared were thirty-eight years until the entire generation of the men, warriors of the camp, had fallen away, as the Lord had sworn to them.
And the hand of God was against them, to eliminate them from the camp until they fell away.

And it happened, when all the men, the warriors, had fallen away, since they were dying off aPossibly presumptuously bI.e. the Raphain cI.e. the sons of omion 1–2 from among the people, 17that the Lord spoke to me, saying,

“Today you are going to pass the boundary of Moab at Aroer, 19and you will draw near to the sons of Amman. Do not act with enmity against them, and do not engage them in battle, for I will not give any of the land of the sons of Amman to you as an allotment, because I have given it to the sons of Lot as an allotment.”

(It will be reckoned as a land of Rephain, for the Rephain formerly inhabited it, and the Ammanites name them Zomzommin, 21a nation, great and numerous and rather powerful, just as the Enakim, and the Lord destroyed them from before them, and they took possession and were settled in place 22 b of them. Just as they did to the sons of Esau, who live in Seir, as theyc wiped out the Chorrite before them, so they took possession and were settled in their place even to this day.
As for the Heuites, who live in Haseroth as far as Gaza, and the Cappadocians, who came from Cappadocia, they wiped them out and were settled in their place.)
“Now then, arise, and depart, and pass through the ravine of Arnon. See, I have given into your hands King Seon the Amorrite of Hesebon and his land. Begin to inherit; engage him in battle.
On this day begin to put your trembling and your fear upon all the nations beneath the sky who, when they hear your name, will be troubled and have pains because of you.”
AndIsentambassadorsfromthewilderness of Kedmoth to King Seon of Hesebon with words of peace, saying:
“I will pass by through your land; I will travel along the road; I will turn neither right nor left.

You will sell me food for money, and I will eat, and you will sell me water for money, and I will drink. Only that I will pass by on foot—29just as the sons of Esau who live in Seir did for me and likewise the Moabites who live in Aroer—until I pass the Jordan into the land that the Lord our God is giving us.”

And King Seon of Hesebon did not want to let us pass by him, for the Lord our God had hardened his spirit and prevailed over his heart in order that he might be delivered into your hands, as it is on this day.
And the Lord said to me, “See, I have begun to give King Seon the Amorrite of Hesebon and his land over before you. Begin to inherit his land.”
And Seon came out to meet us, he and all his people for battle at Iasa, 33and the Lord our God gave him over before us, and we struck him down, and his sons and all his people.
And we captured all the cities at that time, and we utterly destroyed every city in order, and their women and their children we did not leave alive.

Only the cattle we plundered, and the spoils of the cities we took: 36from Aroer, which is along the edge of the Wadi Arnon, and the city that is in the ravine, even as far as the mountain of Galaad. There was not a city which escaped us. The Lord our God gave over all of them into our hands.
We did not enter, however, into the land of the sons of Amman, all the regions bordering the Wadi Iabok as well as the cit- Esau deute ies of the hill country, just as the Lord our God commanded us.
And after we had turned, we went up the r to Basan. And King Og of Basan came o meet us, he and all his people, for battle at Edr

And the Lord said to me, “Do not fear him, have given him over into your hands, and all people and all his land. And you shall do to as you did to King Seon of the Amorrites, used to live in Hesebon.”
And the Lord our delivered him into our hands, even King O Basan and all his people. And we struck him d until we did not leave him offspring.
And at time we captured all his cities; there was no that we did not take from them—sixty cities, al environs of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Ba
All these were strong cities, high walls, gates bars, besides the very many cities of the Pherez
We utterly destroyed them, as we did King Seo Hesebon, and we utterly destroyed every cit order, and the women and the children.
An the cattle and the spoils of the cities we plund for ourselves.
And at that time we took out of the han the two Amorrite kings who were beyond the dan the land from the Wadi Arnon up to Haër
(the Phoenicians named Haërmon Sanior, the Amorrite named it Sanir).
All were citie Misor and all of Galaad and all of Basan, as f Selcha and Edrain, cities of Og’s kingdo Basan.
(Because only King Og of Basan was from the Raphain. Look, his bed was an iron look, it is in the citadel of the sons of Amman aa man’s forearma it is nine cubits long and cubits wide.)
And we took possession of land at that time, from Aroer, which is along edge of the Wadi Arnon, and half of the m tainous area of Galaad and its cities I gav Rouben and to Gad.
And I gave to the halfof Manasse the rest of Galaad and all of Ba Og’s kingdom, and all the environs of Argob, a said Basan. (It will be reckoned a land of Raph
And Iair son of Manasse took all the envi of Argob as far as the borders of Gargasi Homachathi, and he named them after his na Basan Hauoth Iair—until this day.
And Machir I gave Galaad.

And to Rouben an Gad I gave from Galaad as far as the Wadi Ar the middle of the wadi being a boundary, an to the Iabok, the wadi being the boundary for sons of Amman, 17the Araba also and the Jor being a boundary of Machanarath even up to sea of Araba, the Salt Sea, under Asedoth of Ph on the east.

And at that time I commanded you, say “The Lord your God gave you this land as an a ment; proceed fully armed before your brot the sons of Israel, every mighty one of you. 19 your wives and your children and and your tle—I know that you have much cattle—let t settle in your cities that I have given to you 20 aI.e. the common (non-standardized) cubit bour = onomion 3–4 149 ad ad t to in. or I his im ho od of wn hat city the an. nd tes. of in all red s of Joron and of r as in left ed; By our hat the unto ribe an, l of in.) ons and e— to to on, up the an, the sga ng: loters, nly catem ntil the Lord your God gives your brothers rest, as also you and they too will inherit the land that the Lord your God is giving them beyond the Jordan, and you will return, each one to his inheritance that I

have given to you.” And I commanded Iesous at that time, saying: “Your eyes have seen everything that the Lord your God has done to these two kings; so the Lord will do to all the kingdoms which you are about to cross into there.
You shall not be fearful of them, for the Lord your God himself will fight for you.”
At that time, too, I entreated the Lord, saying:

“O Lord, Lord, you have begun to show your attendant your strength and your power and your strong hand and your high arm. For what god is there in the sky or on the earth who will do as you have done, and according to your strength?
After I cross over, therefore, I will see this good land, which is beyond the Jordan, this good mountainous area and the Anti-Lebanon.”
And the Lord ignored me on your account and did not listen to me. And the Lord said to me, “Let it be sufficient for you! Do not continue any longer to speak to me of this matter!
Go up to the top of Hewn, and look with your eyes to the sea and the north and the southwest and the east, and see with your eyes, for you shall not cross over this Jordan.
And command Iesous, and strengthen him, and encourage him, because he will cross over ahead of this people, and it is he who will secure for them the possession of the land that you have seen.”
And we remained in a glen near the house of Phogor.

And now, Israel, hear the statutes and judgments that I am teaching you to do today so that you may live and, after you have entered, may inherit the land that the Lord, the God of your fathers, is giving you.
You shall not add to the word I command you, and you shall not take away from it. Keep the commandments of the Lord your God with which I command you today.

Your eyes have seen all that the Lord yourb God did to Beelphegor—that, every person who went after Beelphegor, the Lord your God wiped him out from among you, 4but those of you who held fast to the Lord your God are all alive today.
See, I have shown you statutes and ordinances, as the Lord has commanded me, to do so in the land into which you are entering there to inherit it.

And you shall keep and do them, because this is your wisdom and discernment before all the nations, as many as might hear all these statutes, and they will say, “Look, this great nation is a wise and understanding people!”
For what sort of great nation has a god for itself so near to them as the Lord our God is in everything whenever we invoke him?
And what sort of great nation has for itself statutes and righteous judgment in accord with all this law that I am giving before you today?

Take heed to yourself, and guard your soul closely, lest you forget all the things that your eyes eed 150 deutero have seen, and let them not be far from your heart all the days of your life, and you shall direct your sons and your sons’ sons 10about the day when you stood before the Lord your God at Choreb, on the day of the assembly when the Lord said to me, “Assemble the people to me, and let them hear my words so that they may learn to fear me all the days as long as they live on the earth and may teach their sons,” 11and you approached and stood at the foot of the mountain, and the mountain was burning with fire up to the sky: darkness, gloom, tempest.
And the Lord spoke to you from the midst of the fire. You heard the sound of words but you did not notice a likeness, only a voice.
And he announced to you his covenant, which he commanded you to do, the ten words, and he wrote them on two stone tablets.
And the Lord commanded me at that time to teach you statutes and ordinances for you to do them in the land into which you are entering there to inherit it.
And guard your souls closely, because you did not notice a likeness on the day the Lord spoke to you at Choreb in the mountain from the midst of the fire.

Do not act lawlessly and make for yourselves an engraved likeness, any kind of icon— a likeness of male or female, 17a likeness of any animal of those that are on the earth, a likeness of any winged bird that flies under the sky, 18a likeness of any reptile that creeps on the ground, a likeness of any fish that is in the waters beneath the earth.

And do not, when you look up to the sky and see the sun and the moon and the stars, even any ornament of the sky, be led astray and do obeisance to them and serve them—those things that the Lord your God has allotted to all the nations beneath the sky.
But God has taken you and brought you out of the iron furnace, out of Egypt, to become for him an allotted people, as in this day.

And the Lord was angry with me because of the things being said by you, and he swore that I would not cross this Jordan and that I would not enter into the land that the Lord God is giving to you as an allotment.
For I am going to die in this land, and I am not crossing over this Jordan, but you are going to cross over, and you will take possession of this good land.

As for you, take care, lest you forget the covenant of the Lord your God, which he made with you, and make for yourselves an engraved likeness of all of the things that the Lord your God has instructed you.
For the Lord your God is a devouring fire, a jealous god.
Now if you bear sons and sons’ sons and live long in the land and you act lawlessly and make an engraved likeness of anything and do what is evil before the Lord your God, to provoke him to anger,

I call sky and earth to witness against you today that by destruction you will perish from the land that you are crossing the Jordan to inherit there; you will not prolong days on it but will be wiped out with a wiping.
And the Lord will scatter you among all the nations, and you will be left few in number among the nations into aLacking in Gk omion 4 which the Lord will bring you there.
And there you will serve other gods, works of human hands, wood and stone, who will neither see nor hear nor eat nor smell.
And there you will seek the Lord your God, and you will find him when you seek him out with your whole heart and with your whole soul, (30) in your distress.
And all these words shall find you in the end of days, and you will return to the Lord your God and listen to his voice.
Because the Lord your God is a compassionate god, he will neither abandon you nor wipe you out; he will not forget the covenant with your fathers that he swore to them.

Ask of former days which occurred long before your own, from the day that God created a human being on the earth; aska at the end of the sky up to the end of the sky whether a thing this great has ever happened, whether such a thing has been heard of; 33whether any nation has ever heard the voice of a living god speaking from the midst of the fire, as you have heard, and you lived, 34whether a god ever attempted to go and take a nation for himself from the midst of a nation, by trial and by signs and by wonders and by war and by a strong hand and a high arm and by great exhibits, like all which the Lord your God did in Egypt before you, as you were looking on 35so that you might know that the Lord your God he is God, and there is no other besides him.
From the sky his voice became audible to discipline you, and on earth he showed you his great fire, and you heard his words coming from the midst of the fire.

Because he loved your fathers, he also chose you, their offspring after them, and himself brought you out of Egypt, by his great power, 38to destroy utterly before you great nations, and mightier than yourselves, to bring you in, to give you their land to inherit, as you have today.
And you will know today and bear in mind that the Lord your God, he is God in the sky above and on the earth beneath, and there is no other besides him.

And you will keep his statutes and his commandments, which I command you today, so that it may be well with you and with your sons after you so that you may be long-lived in the land that the Lord your God is giving you for all your days.

Then Moyses set apart three cities beyond the Jordan toward the rising of the sun 42for the murderer to flee there, whoever might unintentionally kill his neighbor, and he did not hate him before yesterday and the third day, and he shall flee to one of these cities and live:
Bosor in the wilderness on the plain country belonging to Rouben and Ramoth in Galaad belonging to Gaddi and Gaulon in Basan belonging to Manasse.
This is the law that Moyses set before the sons of Israel.

These are the testimonies and the statutes and the judgments that Moyses spoke to the sons of Israel when they had come out of the land of Egypt, 46beyond the Jordan in the valley deute near the house of Phogor, in the land of King S of the Amorrites, who used to live in Hese whoma Moyses and the sons of Israel defe when they had come out of the land of E

And they took possession of his land an land of King Og of Basan, two kings of the A rites who were beyond the Jordan toward the r of the sun, 48from Aroer, which is on the ed the Wadi Arnon, and to Mount Seon (th Haërmon), 49all the Araba beyond the Jo toward the rising of the sun, east under Ase quarried from rock.

And Moyses called all Israel and said to t Hear, O Israel, the statutes and judgments I am speaking in your ears this day, and you s learn them and be watchful to perform them. Lord your God established a covenant with yo Choreb.
Not with your fathers did the Lord es lish this covenant, but with all of you here today.

The Lord spoke with you face-to-face at mountain, from the midst of the fire, 5and I st between the Lord and you at that time, to repo you the words of the Lord, for you were afraid cause of the fire and did not go up into the m tain, saying:
IamtheLordyourGodwhobroughtyo of the land of Egypt, out of a house of slavery. 7 shall have no other gods before me.

You shall not make for yourself a carve ject or likeness of anything whatever is in th above and whatever is in the earth beneath whatever is in the waters under the earth. 9 shall not do obeisance to them, nor are yo serve them, because I am the Lord your God, a ous god, repaying the sins of fathers upon chil to the third and fourth generation to those hate me, 10and doing mercy unto thousands, those who love me and keep my ordinances.
YoushallnottakethenameoftheLord God in vain. For the Lord will never acquit the who takes his name in vain.

Keep the day of the sabbaths to conse it, as the Lord your God commanded you. 1 days you shall labor and do all your labor, 14bu the seventh day there is Sabbata to the Lord God; you shall not do in it any labor—you your son and your daughter, your male slave your female slave, your ox and your draft ani and any animal of yours and the guest within gates so that your male slave and your female s may rest as well as you.

And you shall reme that you were a domestic in the land of Egypt, the Lord your God brought you out from t with a strong hand and with a high arm; there the Lord your God instructed you to keep the of the sabbaths and to consecrate it.
Honor your father and your mother, as Lord your God commanded you, so that it ma well with you and that you may be long-live the land that the Lord your God is giving you.

(17) You shall not murder.
You shall not steal.
You shall not testify falsely against your neighbor with a false testimony.

You shall not covet your neighbor’s wife; you shall not covet your neighbor’s house or his field or his male slave or his female slave or his ox or his draft animal, or any animal of his or whatever belongs to your neighbor.

These words the Lord spoke to your whole gathering at the mountain, from the midst of the fire—darkness, blackness, tempest, a loud voice— and he did not add. And he wrote them on two stone tablets and gave them to me.

And it happened, when you heard the voice from the midst of the fire and the mountain was burning with fire, that you approached me, all the leaders of your tribes and your council of elders, 24and you said, “Look, the Lord our God has shown us his glory, and we have heard his voice from the midst of the fire. This day we have seen that God will speak to a person, and he will live.
And now let us not die. For this great fire will consume us; if we continue to hear the voice of the Lord our God any longer, then we will die.
For what flesh, which has heard the voice of the living God when he speaks from the midst of fire, as we have, shall also live?
Go you near, and hear whatever the Lord our God may say. And you shall speak to us all things, whatever the Lord our God may speak to you, and we will hear and do it.”

AndtheLordheardthesoundofyourwords when you were speaking to me, and the Lord said to me: “I have heard the sound of the words of this people, what they have spoken to you; all that they have spoken bthey have spokenb correctly.
Who shall grant that their heart be in them in such a way that they fear me and keep my commandments in all their days so that it might go well with them and with their sons forever!
Go, say to them, ‘Return to your houses.’

But you, stand here with me, and I will speak to you the commandments and the statutes and the judgments that you shall teach them, and let them do them in the land that I am giving them as an allotment.”

And you shall be watchful to do as the Lord your God has commanded; you shall not turn right or left, 33to go according to all the way that the Lord your God has commanded so that he may give you rest and that it may go well with you and that you may prolong your days upon the land that you shall inherit.

And these are the commandments and the statutes and the ordinances that the Lord your God commanded to teach you to do so in the land which you are entering there to inherit 2so that you may fear the Lord your God, to keep all his statutes and his commandments that I command you today, you and your sons and your sons’ sons, all the days of your life so that you may be longlived.

Now hear, O Israel, and be watchful to per- 152 deutero form so that it may be well with you and that you may multiply greatly, just as the Lord, the God of your fathers, has spoken, to give you a land flowing with milk and honey.
Andthesearethestatutesandthejudgments, which the Lord commanded to the sons of Israel in the wilderness as they were coming out from the land of Egypt. Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God is one Lord.
And you shall love the Lord your God with the whole of your mind and with the whole of your soul and with the whole of your power.
And these words that I command you today shall be in your heart and in your soul.
And you shall teach them to your sons and talk on them while sitting at home and going on the road and lying down and rising up.
And you shall bind them as a sign on your hand, and they shall be athings unshakablea before your eyes, 9and you shall write them on the doorposts of your houses and of your gates.

And it shall be, whenever the Lord your God brings you into the land that he swore to your fathers, to Abraam and Isaak and Iakob, to give you large and fine cities that you did not build, 11houses full of all sorts of goods that you did not fill, dug cisterns that you did not dig out, vineyards and olive groves that you did not plant. And when you eat and become full, 12take care for yourself, lest you forget the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of a house of slavery.
The Lord your God you shall fear, and him you shall serve, and to him you shall cling, and by his name you shall swear.

Do not go after other gods from the gods of the nations around you, 15because the Lord your God, who is present with you, is a jealous god. Lest the Lord your God, bbeing angered with wrath against youb, destroy you utterly from the face of the earth.
You shall not tempt the Lord your God, as you tempted in the Temptation.
By guarding, you shall keep the commandments of the Lord God, the testimonies and the statutes that he has commanded you.

And you shall do what is pleasing and good before the Lord your God, so that it may be well for you and that you may go in and inherit the good land that the Lord swore to your fathers, 19to chase out all your enemies before you, as he said.

And it shall be, when your son asks you tomorrow, saying, “What are the testimonies and the statutes and the judgments that the Lord our God has commanded youc?” 21that you shall say to your son, “We were domestics to Pharao in the land of Egypt, and the Lord brought us from there with a strong hand and with a high arm.
